comments.author: @superkiwistar
comments.text:
LOL. I asked Copilot where the image at 16:33 is. This is the answer:
This photo shows the picturesque town of Christchurch, Dorset, England, not Christchurch in New Zealand. The image captures the River Stour and Christchurch Quay, a popular spot where the river meets the harbour. You can see the Christchurch Sailing Club on the right, the Priory Church further inland, and the green parkland of Christchurch Quay in the centre — all classic landmarks of this charming coastal town near Bournemouth.

comments.text: Amazing place indeed!.... I spent 1year on horesback truout the south island. The most hostile place on the Planet! 250 days of rain, unbearable Winds for days on end...and if you are lucky to see the sun on your trip, you will be devourerd Alive by Sandflys and and bigger than You have ever seen Mosquitos which Sound like drones, that will make you run for shelter. Maybe your car...I was on horseback, ...think again people and by the way, it's about as expensive as Singapore or NY!!! GOOD LUCK

comments.author: @55camille
comments.text:
It’s a shame much of this is inaccurate. We have a beautiful country here. I would have thought footage of other countries, such as Malaysia and Rarotonga belong in their own episodes. Also I would have thought some research into the pronunciation of place names and accurate information on Māori culture would have been easy to find and be less offensive to New Zealanders. 
There is a disproportionate amount of footage of the movie sets from The Lord of the Rings movies, despite them showcasing some of our natural beauty and man made movie sets. 
But , despite that, thank you for bring our treasure to the attention of your viewers. We have a huge variety of landscapes, culture and experiences available here. It’s well worth making the effort to travel to this remote corner of the planet.

replies.author: @TraceTheGlobe
replies.text: Thank you for your thoughtful and detailed feedback. We appreciate you pointing out these concerns, and we understand how important accuracy and cultural representation are when showcasing a country. We're grateful for your perspective and for highlighting the incredible diversity that New Zealand has to offer.

comments.author: @eeeaten
comments.text: if you're thinking of visiting nz, do it, it's great. it's far away and way bigger than you think, so if you can, aim to be there for two weeks or more, half the time in the north island for volcanoes, culture and beaches, half the time in the south for mountains, lakes and glaciers. hot springs are everywhere, food and coffee are good, exchange rate (from europe or us) is good. everyone speaks english, safety generally high, people are friendly and helpful. visit jan-march for the best weather.

replies.author: @TraceTheGlobe
replies.text: Thank you for sharing such helpful advice! We completely agree that New Zealand deserves more than just a quick visit. Your suggested North and South Island split sounds like a fantastic way to experience the country's diversity.

comments.author: @Masque54
comments.text: A reasonably good attempt at portraying the beauty and diversity of New Zealand. It's credibility is diminished, however, by the use of various video segments which are patently not from New Zealand. The most grievous transgression is at 6:28 to 6:42 which is labelled Christchurch but most certainly is not. I think it is perhaps Launceston, Tasmania, Australia.

replies.author: @TraceTheGlobe
replies.text: Thank you for pointing that out. Our goal is always to showcase New Zealand as accurately as possible, and constructive comments like yours help us improve. We appreciate you taking the time to share your observations.
